meta.title: Computation Process For Psychometric Analysis (Item Bank)

introduction.description: This document describes the computation processes of different psychometric analyses. The analysis is system-initiated; the user (Psychometrician) specifies filtering criteria for extracting relevant user data.
introduction.key_concept: Target question is the question whose analysis is performed. All questions undergo analysis separately.

item_difficulty_index_MCQ.description: Item difficulty Index represents the difficulty level of a question experienced by an average end user.

steps: Count total number of responses for target question (Total Count)
Count number of correct responses for target question (Correct Count)
Divide Correct Count by Total Count
Sum up scores of all questions for each user (Total Score)
Find the top 27% of total scores
Count total number of responses for target question in top 27% (Total Count-Top)
Count number of correct responses for target question in top 27% (Correct Count-Top)
Divide Correct Count-Top by Total Count-Top
Sum up scores of all questions for each user (Total Score)
Find the bottom 27% of total scores
Count total number of responses for target question in bottom 27% (Total Count-Bottom)
Count number of correct responses for target question in bottom 27% (Correct Count-Bottom)
Divide Correct Count-Bottom by Total Count-Bottom

item_difficulty_index_non_MCQ.description: Item difficulty Index calculated slightly differently for non-multiple-choice questions.

steps: Sum up scores of all users on the target question (Sum Score-Target)
Divide Sum Score-Target by total number of users [N] to get Mean-Target
Divide Mean-Target by maximum score for the target question
Sum up scores of all questions for each user (Total Score)
Find the top 27% total scores
Sum up scores of target question for users in top 27% (Sum Score-Top Target)
Divide Sum Score-Top Target by number of users in top 27% [N-T] to get Mean-Top Target
Divide Mean-Top Target by maximum score for the target question
top_group_difficulty_index_UDI.formula: UDI = (Sum Score-Top Target / N-T) / Max Score

steps: Sum up scores of all questions for each user (Total Score)
Find bottom 27% of total scores
Sum up scores of target question for users in bottom 27% (Sum Score-Bottom Target)
Divide Sum Score-Bottom Target by number of users in bottom 27% [N-B] to get Mean-Bottom Target
Divide Mean-Bottom Target by maximum score for the target question
bottom_group_difficulty_index_BDI.formula: BDI = (Sum Score-Bottom Target / N-B) / Max Score

item_discrimination_index_MCQ.description: Item discrimination Index signifies the psychometric validity of a question. Calculated per question separately.
steps: Sum up scores of all questions EXCEPT target question for each user (Total Score)
Sum up total scores of all users (Summary Score)
Find mean: Summary Score / total number of users [N] = Mean Score
Subtract mean score from each summary score and square the output (Squared Difference)
Sum up squared differences (Total Squared Difference [TSD])
Divide TSD by N and find square root → Standard Deviation [SD]
Sum up total scores of users who got target question CORRECT (Summary Score-Correct)
Divide Summary Score-Correct by number of users who got target correct [N1] = Mean-Correct
Sum up total scores of users who got target question INCORRECT (Summary Score-Incorrect)
Divide Summary Score-Incorrect by number of users who got target incorrect [N2] = Mean-Incorrect
Divide N1 by N → N1Quotient
Divide N2 by N → N2Quotient
Multiply N1Quotient by N2Quotient and find square root → N-Coefficient
Subtract Mean-Incorrect from Mean-Correct and multiply by N-Coefficient → Mean-Coefficient
Divide Mean-Coefficient by SD → Item Discrimination Index
item_discrimination_index_MCQ.formula: Discrimination Index = ((Mean-Correct - Mean-Incorrect) × sqrt(N1/N × N2/N)) / SD

item_total_correlation_index_non_MCQ.description: For non-MCQ, item total correlation index is the analysis performed.
item_total_correlation_index_non_MCQ.note: System pulls out only data of users who have attempted ALL questions in the list.
steps: Sum up scores of all questions EXCEPT target question for each user (Total Score)
Sum up total scores of all users (Summary Score)
Divide Summary Score by total number of users [N] → Mean-Total
Subtract Mean-Total from each summary score → Difference-Total
Square each Difference-Total value (Squared Difference)
Sum up squared differences (Total Squared Difference [TSD])
Divide TSD by N and find square root → Standard Deviation-Total [SD-Total]
Sum up scores of all users for target question and divide by N → Mean-Target
Subtract Mean-Target from each user's target question score → Difference-Target
Square each Difference-Target (Squared Difference-Target)
Sum up Squared Differences-Target (Total Squared Difference-Target [TSD-T])
Divide TSD-T by N and find square root → Standard Deviation-Target [SD-T]
Multiply Difference-Total for each user by Difference-Target for same user → Difference-Product
Sum up Difference-Product of all users (Sum Difference-Product)
Multiply SD-Total by SD-Target and multiply by N → SD-N-Product
Divide Sum Difference-Product by SD-N-Product → Item Total Correlation Index
item_total_correlation_index_non_MCQ.formula: Item Total Correlation = Sum(Difference-Total × Difference-Target) / (SD-Total × SD-Target × N)
